name: Reframe AltID's procurement strategy
consequences: Engaging in this lever will lead to a direct effect of redefining the procurement process for AltID, with downstream implications such as increased scrutiny from suppliers and potential delays in project timelines.
options: Prioritize open standards and platform-neutral requirements
Establish a transparent evaluation framework for suppliers
Develop a phased implementation plan for supplier diversification
review: This lever governs the tension between procurement neutrality and operational continuity, but the options overlook potential costs associated with retraining existing staff or adapting internal processes.

name: Develop a platform-neutral fallback authentication path
consequences: Implementing this lever will lead to a direct effect of creating an alternative authentication method, with downstream implications such as potential security risks if not properly implemented.
options: Integrate FIDO2/WebAuthn and OpenID Connect on GrapheneOS
Explore browser-based or hardware-token-based fallback authentication
Develop a hybrid approach combining mobile and desktop authentication
review: This lever governs the tension between security and usability, but the options overlook potential issues related to device compatibility or user acceptance.

name: Engage with EU bodies and technical working groups
consequences: Participating in this lever will lead to a direct effect of influencing European Digital Identity Wallet requirements, with downstream implications such as potential changes to existing digital identity frameworks.
options: Align Danish advocacy with broader European requirements on interoperability
Advocate for platform-neutral access and certified fallback mechanisms
Develop a joint proposal with other EU countries for enhanced digital identity standards
review: This lever governs the tension between national sovereignty and international cooperation, but the options overlook potential challenges related to coordination among different EU member states.

name: Establish a coalition of stakeholders
consequences: Building this lever will lead to a direct effect of creating a collective voice for change, with downstream implications such as increased influence on policy decisions.
options: Build a coalition spanning civil society organizations and academic researchers
Engage with sympathetic members of Folketinget's digital policy committee
Develop a public awareness campaign to educate citizens about the benefits of platform-neutral access
review: This lever governs the tension between internal and external influence, but the options overlook potential challenges related to maintaining coalition cohesion or engaging with skeptical stakeholders.

name: Develop a phased implementation plan
consequences: Creating this lever will lead to a direct effect of defining a step-by-step approach for change, with downstream implications such as potential delays in project timelines.
options: Develop a three-phase plan for procurement intervention and standards pressure
Establish a timeline for technical demonstration and EU engagement
Create a contingency plan for potential setbacks or delays
review: This lever governs the tension between flexibility and predictability, but the options overlook potential challenges related to maintaining stakeholder buy-in or adapting to changing circumstances.

name: Secure funding for contingency capacity
consequences: Allocating this lever will lead to a direct effect of ensuring financial resources are available, with downstream implications such as potential changes to project scope or timelines.
options: Budget for institutional overhead and external security review
Allocate funds for limited legal/procurement support
Develop a contingency plan for potential funding shortfalls
review: This lever governs the tension between financial stability and operational flexibility, but the options overlook potential challenges related to maintaining stakeholder buy-in or adapting to changing circumstances.

name: Review and revise project goals
consequences: Revisiting this lever will lead to a direct effect of reassessing project objectives, with downstream implications such as potential changes to project scope or timelines.
options: Reassess the feasibility report and demonstrators' impact on procurement decisions
Revise the project's EU-level engagement goals and strategies
Develop a contingency plan for potential setbacks or delays
review: This lever governs the tension between adaptability and accountability, but the options overlook potential challenges related to maintaining stakeholder buy-in or adapting to changing circumstances.

name: Engage with Danish parliamentarians on digital sovereignty
consequences: This lever governs the tension between centralization and local autonomy, but the options overlook transition costs.
options: Establish a parliamentary task force to investigate platform dependence in Danish public services
Host workshops for parliamentarians on the implications of digital sovereignty for Denmark's economic competitiveness
Support a cross-party coalition to draft legislation mandating platform-neutral access requirements
review: This lever governs the tension between centralization and local autonomy, but the options overlook transition costs.

name: Develop a digital identity resilience framework
consequences: Prioritizing speed over reliability carries hidden costs: none of the options address rollback complexity.
options: Create a public-private partnership to develop and publish a comprehensive risk assessment methodology for Danish digital identity systems
Establish a working group with industry stakeholders to identify best practices for continuity planning in the face of platform disruptions
Publish a white paper outlining the economic benefits of investing in resilience-focused digital identity infrastructure
review: Prioritizing speed over reliability carries hidden costs: none of the options address rollback complexity.

name: Influence procurement language for AltID
consequences: This lever governs the tension between centralization and local autonomy, but the options overlook transition costs.
options: Draft and publish a set of recommended procurement requirements that prioritize platform neutrality and fallback access capabilities
Support the development of an industry-led consortium to promote supplier-neutral design standards for Danish digital identity systems
Advocate for the inclusion of platform-neutrality language in official AltID-related design, policy, consultation, or procurement documents
review: This lever governs the tension between centralization and local autonomy, but the options overlook transition costs.

name: Engage with EU bodies on digital identity standards
consequences: The project's role is not to dictate architecture directly, but to create technical evidence, procurement language, and standards pressure that make supplier-neutral and fallback-capable design harder to exclude.
options: Support the development of a European Digital Identity Wallet framework that prioritizes platform neutrality and fallback access capabilities
Engage with relevant EU bodies and technical working groups connected to the European Digital Identity Wallet framework
Advocate for the inclusion of platform-neutrality language in official EU digital identity standards documents
review: The project's role is not to dictate architecture directly, but to create technical evidence, procurement language, and standards pressure that make supplier-neutral and fallback-capable design harder to exclude.

name: Develop a phased implementation plan for AltID
consequences: This lever governs the tension between centralization and local autonomy, but the options overlook transition costs.
options: Create a detailed project schedule outlining key milestones and timelines for implementing platform-neutral access requirements in AltID
Support the development of an industry-led consortium to promote supplier-neutral design standards for Danish digital identity systems
Advocate for the inclusion of platform-neutrality language in official AltID-related design, policy, consultation, or procurement documents
review: This lever governs the tension between centralization and local autonomy, but the options overlook transition costs.
